segments.text: Oh, that you would bear with me in a little folly—and indeed you do bear with me.
segments.speaker: Paul

footnotes: * Likely a textual marker or emphasis

segments.text: For I am jealous for you with godly jealousy. For I have betrothed you to one husband, that I may present you as a chaste virgin to Christ.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: But I fear, lest somehow, as the serpent deceived Eve by his craftiness, so your minds may be corrupted from the simplicity that is in Christ.

For if he who comes preaches another Jesus whom we have not preached, or if you receive a different spirit which you have not received, or a different gospel which you have not accepted—you may well put up with it!
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: For I consider that I am not at all inferior to the most eminent apostles.
segments.speaker: Paul

footnotes: * Likely a textual marker or emphasis

segments.text: Even though I am untrained in speech, yet I am not in knowledge. But we have been thoroughly manifested among you in all things.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Did I commit sin in humbling myself that you might be exalted, because I preached the gospel of God to you free of charge?
segments.speaker: Paul

footnotes: * Likely a textual marker or emphasis

segments.text: I robbed other churches, taking wages from them to minister to you.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: And when I was present with you, and in need, I was a burden to no one, for what I lacked the brethren who came from Macedonia supplied. And in everything I kept myself from being burdensome to you, and so I will keep myself.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: As the truth of Christ is in me, no one shall stop me from this boasting in the regions of Achaia.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Why? Because I do not love you? God knows!
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: But what I do, I will also continue to do, that I may cut off the opportunity from those who desire an opportunity to be regarded just as we are in the things of which they boast.
segments.speaker: Paul

footnotes: * Likely a textual marker or emphasis

segments.text: For such are false apostles, deceitful workers, transforming themselves into apostles of Christ.
And no wonder! For Satan himself transforms himself into an angel of light.
Therefore it is no great thing if his ministers also transform themselves into ministers of righteousness, whose end will be according to their works.
I say again, let no one think me a fool. If otherwise, at least receive me as a fool, that I also may boast a little.
segments.speaker: Paul

footnotes: * Likely a textual marker or emphasis

segments.text: What I speak, I speak not according to the Lord, but as it were, foolishly, in this confidence of boasting.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Seeing that many boast according to the flesh, I also will boast.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: For you put up with fools gladly, since you yourselves are wise!
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: For you put up with it if one brings you into bondage, if one devours you, if one takes from you, if one exalts himself, if one strikes you on the face.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: To our shame I say that we were too weak for that! But in whatever anyone is bold—I speak foolishly—I am bold also.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Are they Hebrews? So am I. Are they Israelites? So am I. Are they the seed of Abraham? So am I.
segments.speaker: Paul

footnotes: * Likely a textual marker or emphasis

segments.text: Are they ministers of Christ?—I speak as a fool—I am more: in labors more abundant, in stripes above measure, in prisons more frequently, in deaths often.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: From the Jews five times I received forty stripes minus one.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Three times I was beaten with rods; once I was stoned; three times I was shipwrecked; a night and a day I have been in the deep;
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: in journeys often, in perils of waters, in perils of robbers, in perils of my own countrymen, in perils of the Gentiles, in perils in the city, in perils in the wilderness, in perils in the sea, in perils among false brethren;
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: in weariness and toil, in sleeplessness often, in hunger and thirst, in fastings often, in cold and nakedness—
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: besides the other things, what comes upon me daily: my deep concern for all the churches.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Who is weak, and I am not weak? Who is made to stumble, and I do not burn with indignation?
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: If I must boast, I will boast in the things which concern my infirmity.
segments.speaker: Paul

footnotes: * Likely a textual marker or emphasis

segments.text: The God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, who is blessed forever, knows that I am not lying.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: In Damascus the governor, under Aretas the king, was guarding the city of the Damascenes with a garrison, desiring to arrest me;
but I was let down in a basket through a window in the wall, and escaped from his hands.
